items.title: Cisco Patches Sixth SD-WAN Zero-Day of 2026 — CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS 7.8) Actively Exploited
items.summary: Cisco disclosed CVE-2026-20245, a command injection vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager. An attacker with netadmin privileges can upload a crafted file and execute arbitrary commands as root. This marks the sixth Cisco SD-WAN zero-day to be actively exploited in 2026, pointing to sustained, targeted attacker interest in SD-WAN infrastructure.
items.why: Six actively exploited SD-WAN zero-days in a single year from one vendor signals systematic pre-positioned access to enterprise network infrastructure. Any org running Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN should treat this as an emergency patch given the established exploitation pattern.

items.title: CISA Adds Magento RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45247 (CVSS 9.8) to KEV Catalog — Unauthenticated Code Execution
items.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-45247 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 3, 2026. The flaw is a deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability in the Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer extension for Magento, allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code on affected e-commerce servers. Patches are available in version 1.11.12+.
items.why: Unauthenticated RCE against e-commerce platforms with confirmed active exploitation. Attackers targeting Magento stores can achieve full server compromise, steal payment data, and inject payment skimmers. Any merchant running the affected extension must patch immediately.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours of Disclosure, Uses Cloud C2
items.summary: Trellix researchers uncovered a multi-stage espionage campaign by Russia's APT28 (Fancy Bear) that weaponized Microsoft Office vulnerability CVE-2026-21509 within 24 hours of its public disclosure. The campaign targets European military and government entities, using legitimate cloud services for command-and-control infrastructure to blend with normal traffic and evade detection.
items.why: Demonstrates nation-state actors operating inside the patch window — exploiting a disclosed vuln before defenders can respond. Cloud C2 abuse is a mature evasion tactic that defeats perimeter-based detection. European defense and government orgs should review IOCs and hunt for lateral movement immediately.

items.title: Unit 42 Tracks Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Deploying Six New RAT Variants Across 5 Countries
items.summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 is tracking Iranian threat actor 'Screening Serpens,' which intensified operations following a regional conflict in February 2026. Between February and April 2026, the group deployed six new RAT variants split across two malware families — MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 — against targets across up to five countries. The rapid malware iteration indicates a well-resourced development pipeline adapting to detection.
items.why: Six distinct RAT variants in three months is an unusually fast development cadence, suggesting the actor is actively evading detection signatures. Organizations in the Middle East, Europe, and government/defense sectors should update threat feeds with the new MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 indicators.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Network Access in 22 Seconds, AI-Enabled Malware PROMPTFLUX Evades Defenses
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, drawn from 500,000+ hours of frontline IR investigations, documents that attackers now hand off compromised network access to secondary threat actors in just 22 seconds (down from 8+ hours in 2022). New malware families PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL query LLMs mid-execution to dynamically evade detection, while the QUIETVAULT credential stealer scans for local AI tools. Exploits remain the leading initial access vector for the sixth consecutive year.
items.why: A 22-second hand-off window collapses traditional detection-and-response timelines entirely. AI-native malware that consults LLMs for evasion represents a qualitative shift defenders have not encountered at scale before. Every SOC and IR team should recalibrate their playbooks against these findings.
